differences.title: 1. INITIAL LOAD
differences.leftDesc: Qwik loads minimal JS upfront, making initial loads incredibly fast and interactive.
differences.rightDesc: SolidJS delivers minimal JavaScript, optimizing runtime performance with efficient reactivity.

differences.title: 2. REACTIVITY
differences.leftDesc: Qwik's reactivity is optimized for resumability, avoiding complex dependency tracking.
differences.rightDesc: SolidJS uses fine-grained reactivity, similar to Vue's Composition API, for efficient updates.

differences.title: 3. STATE MANAGEMENT
differences.leftDesc: Qwik manages state with simple variables and its `useStore` hook.
differences.rightDesc: SolidJS leverages signals for reactive state management, updating only what's needed.

differences.title: 4. RENDERING
differences.leftDesc: Qwik renders on the server first, then resumes on the client without re-executing.
differences.rightDesc: SolidJS renders primarily on the client, leveraging its reactivity for efficient updates.

differences.title: 5. BUNDLING
differences.leftDesc: Qwik supports lazy loading of components and code automatically.
differences.rightDesc: SolidJS focuses on minimal bundles with fine-grained control over dependencies.

differences.title: 6. COMPATIBILITY
differences.leftDesc: Qwik's syntax feels familiar to React developers, with a twist.
differences.rightDesc: SolidJS uses JSX and offers a familiar developer experience for React devs.

differences.title: 7. RESUMABILITY
differences.leftDesc: Qwik's defining feature is resumability, eliminating hydration completely.
differences.rightDesc: SolidJS prioritizes performance and reactivity, not direct resumability.
